summary: nolimit-x ships an obfuscated phishing / SMS-bombing / mailbox-contact-harvesting toolkit with plaintext host fingerprint phone-home
details: The package advertises itself as an 'Advanced email sender' with the 'red-team' keyword and ships its entire runtime under.ad/ (45+ files) as javascript-obfuscator output (RC4 string array, self-defending regex, no original source) — concealing the actual behavior from reviewers. Decoded strings show the CLI provides: (a) Microsoft Graph and IMAP mailbox contact harvesting (extractContacts, extractContactsIMAP in x12.js); (b) bulk SMS bombing via carrier SMTP-to-SMS gateways (sendViaSmtpGateway, lookupCarrier, getGatewayEmail in x13.js); (c) OAuth2 device-code-flow token capture against Microsoft/Google (deviceCodeAuth in x3.js); (d) DKIM key generation and SPF spoofing record output to support sender impersonation. In addition, x4.js hardcodes LICENSE_SERVER='http://api.nolimitent.xyz:4100' and the activate/verify commands POST {key, hwid, hostname, platform} over plaintext HTTP to api.nolimitent.xyz, leaking the installer's host fingerprint to the author and to any on-path observer. The postinstall.js hook is benign (Windows bin-shim only), but the package's purpose is offensive operations against third-party mailbox owners and SMS recipients, packaged behind heavy obfuscation. Installing this in CI, shared development environments, or pipelines that auto-resolve dependencies places an attack toolkit on the host and exposes installer identifiers to the author over cleartext.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
